behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is OS watcher behavior (recursive vs non-recursive directory watching) plus daemon wiring. A mock watcher can hide the real failure where subdirectory events are never emitted by the platform/backend. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely filesystem/watch logic with no AI/ML decision and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, write a file there, and assert exactly one transformed output appears in the output directory without restart.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily cross-module state handoff (request intake -> dedup window/cache -> pipeline trigger). Real OS/network/subprocess is not required if duplicate requests are injected at module boundaries; fake clock/time control can expose boundary bugs. Rejected System based on behavior text: it specifies duplicate processing requests by path+window, not OS-specific event semantics. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML dependency in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake, deduper, and processing dispatcher in-process with a controllable clock; submit the same file path twice inside the configured window and assert only one processing execution, then outside the window and assert processing occurs again.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real OS multi-event emission for one write. In-process fakes can miss backend-specific duplicate event patterns that trigger real duplicate processing bugs. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output quality or multi-agent chain is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real watched directory, perform a write pattern known to produce multiple events on the host OS, and verify only one pipeline execution/output is produced for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is real filesystem lifecycle behavior (directory deletion/recreation) and watcher reattachment under OS notifications. Mocking watcher events can hide failures in real re-subscription behavior. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no model-driven decision or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon continuously, delete the watched directory, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed successfully without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on concurrent arrival and filesystem timestamp semantics under real event timing; mocks can hide race/order inversions caused by OS event delivery and timestamp granularity. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior text is deterministic filesystem ordering, not AI/ML output.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple files nearly simultaneously in the watched tree using real filesystem writes, capture processing order from daemon outputs/logs, and assert it matches creation-timestamp order (including deterministic tie-break rule if equal timestamps).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This requires real partial-write timing and real watcher/read behavior; in-process stubs often cannot reproduce lock/flush/close timing that causes premature processing in production. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is filesystem readiness gating, with no AI/ML-dependent correctness or multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running, open a file in the watched directory, write in chunks with delays before close, trigger arrival event naturally, and assert processing starts only after final write/close and uses complete file contents.
